```json { "module": "auth-rbac", "scores": { "visual_polish": 42, "domain_depth": 48, "data_richness": 35, "interaction_quality": 38, "ai_integration": 5, "mobile_first": 30, "performance": 45, "typescript_quality": 52, "error_handling": 40, "realtime": 20, "accessibility": 25, "security": 55, "differentiation": 30, "enterprise_readiness": 40 }, "overall_score": 36, "issues": [ { "id": "auth-rbac-C1-001", "severity": "critical", "dimension": "security", "title": "ProtectedRoute file is truncated — permission check and render logic incomplete", "file": "src/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx", "description": "The ProtectedRoute component is cut off mid-code. The module-level permission check via canAccessModule is never completed, and the final return statement that renders children is missing. This means the component either fails to compile or silently allows access to all authenticated users regardless of permissions.", "prompt": "In the file src/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx, in the ProtectedRoute component: complete the truncated permission check block. BEFORE: The code is cut off after the comment '// Check module-level permission' with no closing logic or final return statement rendering children. AFTER: Add the moduleId permission check: `if (moduleId && !canAccessModule(moduleId)) { return ; }`. Then close the permLoading block with `}`. Finally add the default return: `return <>{children}`. Ensure the component function and the file are properly closed with all braces balanced. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors." }, { "id": "auth-rbac-C1-002", "severity": "critical", "dimension": "security", "title": "PermissionGate.tsx is truncated — RequestAccessPage component incomplete", "file": "src/components/auth/PermissionGate.tsx", "description": "The PermissionGate file is cut off inside the RequestAccessPage component's JSX. The h2 style object is incomplete, and the rest of the component (role display, request access button, closing divs, and export) is missing entirely. This causes a build failure.", "prompt": "In the file src/components/auth/PermissionGate.tsx, in the RequestAccessPage component: complete the truncated JSX. BEFORE: The code is cut off mid-style-object inside the h2 element of RequestAccessPage. AFTER: Complete the h2 with `color: colors.textPrimary, marginBottom: spacing['2'] }}>Access Restricted`. Add a paragraph: `

{moduleName ? \\`You do not have permission to access ${moduleName}. Contact your project administrator to request access.\\` : 'You do not have permission to access this page. Contact your project administrator to request access.'}

`. Add role display: `{role &&

Your current role: {role}

}`. Close all divs. Ensure the file exports both PermissionGate and RequestAccessPage. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors." }, { "id": "auth-rbac-C1-003", "severity": "critical", "dimension": "error_handling", "title": "Login page missing loading state, error display, and proper error mapping", "file": "src/pages/Login.tsx", "description": "Based on AUTH-002 and AUTH-007 acceptance criteria, the login page must show user-friendly mapped error messages (never raw Supabase errors like 'invalid_credentials' or 'AuthApiError'), display a loading/disabled state on the submit button during auth, and handle network errors gracefully. These are P0 acceptance criteria violations.", "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Login.tsx, in the login form component: add error mapping and loading states. BEFORE: Auth errors from Supabase are likely displayed raw or not displayed at all. The submit button has no loading/disabled state during authentication. AFTER: 1) Add an error mapping function at the top of the file: `function mapAuthError(msg: string): string { const m = msg.toLowerCase(); if (m.includes('invalid login') || m.includes('invalid_credentials')) return 'Email or password is incorrect'; if (m.includes('email not confirmed')) return 'Please check your email to confirm your account'; if (m.includes('rate limit') || m.includes('too many')) return 'Too many attempts. Please try again in a few minutes'; if (m.includes('fetch') || m.includes('network') || m.includes('failed')) return 'Unable to connect. Check your internet connection'; if (m.includes('already registered') || m.includes('already been registered')) return 'An account with this email already exists'; return msg; }`. 2) Wrap the auth error state through this function before displaying. 3) Add `disabled={loading}` and a spinner or 'Signing in...' text to the submit button while auth is in progress. 4) Display the mapped error in a styled div with role='alert' and aria-live='polite'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors." }, { "id": "auth-rbac-C1-004", "severity": "major", "dimension": "accessibility", "title": "Login form missing ARIA attributes, labels, and keyboard handling", "file": "src/pages/Login.tsx", "description": "Per Global Acceptance Standards: all form inputs need associated labels (not placeholders as labels), error states need aria-live regions, the form needs proper aria-describedby for errors, and focus management after errors. The login page is the first thing every user sees — accessibility here is non-negotiable.", "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Login.tsx, in the login form JSX: add proper ARIA attributes. BEFORE: Form inputs likely use placeholder text only without explicit
